title: Antibiotic Resistance
definition: Bacteria's ability to survive antibiotic exposure through evolutionary mechanisms.

difficulty_levels.beginner: When bacteria adapt and become immune to certain antibiotics, making them less effective.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A phenomenon arising from bacterial evolution in response to selective pressure exerted by antibiotic use, leading to increased treatment failures.
difficulty_levels.expert: The emergence of resistance mechanisms in bacteria as a result of overuse and misuse of antibiotics, accelerated by genetic transfer, posing significant threats to human health due to the diminishing efficacy of current treatments.

extra_fields.insight: The relentless evolutionary arms race between bacteria and humanity's medical arsenal.
